heading: Configure VPC Peering for a GCP-backed Cluster
body:
To configure Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) Peering for a Google Cloud-backed
cluster:

> **Note:** You can skip this step if you are using the Atlas CLI to add a network peering connection.

- In Atlas, go to the Database & Network Access page for your project.

  - If it's not already displayed, select the
    organization that contains your project from the
    Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

  - If it's not already displayed, select your project
    from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

  - In the sidebar, click Database & Network Access under
    the Security heading.

    The [Database & Network Access](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fsecurity%2Fnetwork%2FaccessList)
    page displays.

- In the Peering tab, click 
  Add Peering Connection.

> **Note:** Since Google Cloud uses global VPCs, you need to create only one peering connection.

Atlas CLI

To create a network peering connection with Google Cloud using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas networking peering create gcp [options]

```

To watch for a peering connection to become available using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas networking peering watch <peerId> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas networking peering create gcp](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-networking-peering-create-gcp) and
[atlas networking peering watch](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-networking-peering-watch).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

Atlas UI

- In the Peering Connection modal, select
  Google Cloud Platform and click
  Next.

- To create the VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) Peering connection, fill in the
  requested information in the Peering Connection modal:

  | Field      | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
  | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Project ID | Google Cloud Project ID of the peer VPC (Virtual Private Cloud). Refer to the dialog box for instructions on finding your GCP Project ID.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
  | VPC Name   | Name of the peer VPC (Virtual Private Cloud). Refer to the dialog box for instructions on finding your VPC Name.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
  | Atlas CIDR | CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block for your Atlas cluster.Atlas uses the specified CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block for all other network peering connections created in the project. By default, the Atlas CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block must be at least an /18 in one of the following private networks.Lower BoundUpper BoundPrefix10.0.0.010.255.255.25510/8172.16.0.0172.31.255.255172.16/12192.168.0.0192.168.255.255192.168/16If your application requires Atlas to use a smaller CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block, use the Atlas API to create an Atlas network peering container with a CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block of /21 to /24.When you choose a smaller CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block, the IP address space of the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block you choose is distributed evenly across the Google Cloud regions to which you deploy the network peering container. Atlas requires a CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block of /24 for each region. Refer to the following table to learn the number of regions to which you can deploy a network peering container based on the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block you choose.CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) BlockNumber of Google Cloud Regions/211 - 8/221 - 4/231 - 2/241IMPORTANT: You can't use the Atlas user interface to specify an Atlas CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block smaller than /18. You must use the Atlas API and specify the regions (up to eight, based on the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block you choose) to which to deploy the network peering container. You can deploy Atlas clusters only to these regions in this project.Atlas locks this value for all regions if an M10 or greater cluster or a network peering connection already exists in that project.To modify the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block, the target project cannot have:Any M10 or greater clustersAny other VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connectionsAlternatively, create a new project and create a network peering connection to set the desired Atlas network peering CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block for that project.IMPORTANT: Atlas limits the number of MongoDB nodes per network peering connection based on the CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block and the region selected for the project. For example, a project with an Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block of /18 is limited to approximately 80 three-node replica sets per Google Cloud region. Contact MongoDB Support for any questions on Atlas limits of MongoDB nodes per network peering connection. |

- Click Initiate Peering.

* In the Google Cloud Console, click
  VPC network peering.

* Click Create Connection.

* Click Continue.

* In Name, enter a name for your peering
  connection.

* In Your VPC Network, enter the name of your
  Google Cloud VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) network.

* In Peered VPC network, select
  In another project.

* In Project ID, enter your Atlas GCP Project ID.

  To find this name in the VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) Peering view in Atlas. In
  the Security section of the left navigation:

  - In Atlas, go to the Database & Network Access page for your project.

    - If it's not already displayed, select the
      organization that contains your project from the
      Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

    - If it's not already displayed, select your project
      from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

    - In the sidebar, click Database & Network Access under
      the Security heading.

      The [Database & Network Access](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fsecurity%2Fnetwork%2FaccessList)
      page displays.

  - Click the Peering tab.

* In VPC network name, enter your Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
  Name.

  To find this name in the VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) Peering view in Atlas:

  - In Atlas, go to the Database & Network Access page for your project.

    - If it's not already displayed, select the
      organization that contains your project from the
      Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

    - If it's not already displayed, select your project
      from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

    - In the sidebar, click Database & Network Access under
      the Security heading.

      The [Database & Network Access](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fsecurity%2Fnetwork%2FaccessList)
      page displays.

  - Click the Peering tab.

> **Note:** Each Atlas project may have a maximum of 50 peering connections
> in total. This total includes a maximum of 25 pending
> peering connections.

You must add your VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block address (or subset)
associated with the peer VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) to the
[IP Access List](access-list) before your new VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peer
can connect to your Atlas cluster. When connecting to your
cluster, you must use the new [private connection strings](atlas-faq-connstring-private) to utilize the peering.

> **See Also:** [Auto mode IP ranges](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/vpc#ip-ranges).
